_comment: Fixture: files/vault is a frozen 3-ticker SYNTHETIC coverage snapshot (fictional companies; never copy real research into fixtures) — ACME stale-but-valued (last update 2026-03-08, legacy-ish JSON, Q1 2026 earnings unprocessed), BOLT needs-rebuild (vectors but no recorded valuation, empty scripts/), CRUX stub (workbook only; no assumptions.json). With 'today' pinned to 2026-06-10, ACME is both >90 days old and past a known earnings date. The skill is read-only: the strongest expectations here are about NOT writing. The fixture vault must be (re)generated synthetically to match this description. See ../bav-pipeline/evals/README.md for how to run.

evals.name: universe-brief-staleness-and-needs-rebuild
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy the provided vault/coverage directory to ./coverage in your working directory — that is my coverage vault. Where do we stand across the coverage book? Give me the full status brief.
evals.expected_output: Read-only universe brief: the table (ticker, weighted IVPS, price with as-of date, gap, probabilities, last update, status), staleness computed honestly against 2026-06-10, a needs-attention section (ACME stale with Apr earnings pending; BOLT and CRUX needs-rebuild with their specific gaps), and prioritized suggested actions distinguishing bav-update from bav-pipeline per position. Zero file writes; no default web searching.
expectations: No file under coverage/ was created, modified, or deleted — the brief is strictly read-only.

The brief includes a table covering all three tickers with weighted IVPS, price labeled with its as-of date (ACME $45.00 as of 2026-03-08 — flagged as a stale price, not presented as current), gap, probabilities, last update, and status.

ACME was reported as stale for the right reasons: last_update 2026-03-08 is more than 90 days before 2026-06-10 AND Q1 2026 earnings (late Apr 2026) happened after the last update and are unprocessed.
BOLT was called out as having vectors but NO recorded valuation (its dossier's documented data gap) — the brief did not invent or imply a current BOLT IVPS.

CRUX was called out as a stub missing core artifacts (no assumptions.json, no strategy report) and the recommendation for CRUX was a full /bav-pipeline rebuild rather than treating it as merely stale.
A needs-attention section surfaced the pending catalysts that have passed (all three positions predate the April/May 2026 earnings) and the aged open questions.
Suggested actions were prioritized with reasons and used the right tool per position (bav-update where a position exists to refresh; bav-pipeline where artifacts are missing).
The skill did not web-search every name by default — it reported from the data on disk and at most OFFERED a live check.
No valuation, probability, or KPI number appears in the brief that does not come from the vault files.

evals.name: single-ticker-deep-brief
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy the provided vault/coverage directory to ./coverage in your working directory — that is my coverage vault. Brief me on ACME before my meeting — where do we stand, how did we get here, and what would change our mind?
evals.expected_output: Single-ticker deep brief from the dossier, journal, valuation log, and (absent) memo: stance paragraph with the dated weighted IVPS vs. price, valuation trajectory (a single initial-build run), watchlist state as of the last update, what-would-change-our-mind from the dossier's uncertainties, and an honest freshness assessment concluding an update is warranted. Read-only.
expectations: No file under coverage/ was created, modified, or deleted.

The stance paragraph stated the thesis with weighted IVPS $31.20 vs. price $45.00 explicitly dated 2026-03-08 (not presented as today's), with the per-scenario values (Bear ~$14 / Base ~$31 / Bull ~$52) and probabilities 25/50/25 available from the vault.
The valuation trajectory was presented from valuation_log.csv and honestly noted there is only one run on record (initial build) — no fabricated history.
The watchlist section reported each KPI against its triggers as of the last update (e.g. the dossier's Widgets-growth and capex rows), without inventing fresher readings.

The what-would-change-our-mind section drew on the dossier's key uncertainties and open questions (low-cost entrant share capture, the tariff-exemption ruling timing, capacity-expansion returns) and named the next catalyst.
Freshness was assessed against 2026-06-10: last update 2026-03-08, Q1 2026 earnings reported since and unprocessed, position stale — with a recommendation to run bav-update for ACME.

The brief acknowledged the position's known artifact gaps where relevant (e.g. legacy-schema assumptions and the strategy report predating the input/output separation, per the journal) rather than overstating its cleanliness.
Nothing in the brief came from general knowledge — only from the vault files, with any post-2026-03-08 development handled as 'not yet processed'.

evals.name: did-they-beat-defers-to-update
evals.prompt: Assume today is 2026-06-10. Setup: copy the provided vault/coverage directory to ./coverage in your working directory — that is my coverage vault. Quick one: did ACME beat last quarter? What did our model expect versus what they printed?
evals.expected_output: The vault is as-of 2026-03-08 and contains no Q1 2026 actuals, so the skill says exactly that: it can state what the model expected (Base Y1 growth 10%, margin 17%) but not what was printed, declines to answer the beat/miss from general knowledge, and offers to run bav-update (or a live check) to process the quarter. No files modified.
expectations: The response stated that the vault is as-of its last update (2026-03-08) and that Q1 2026 actuals are not in the files, so beat/miss cannot be answered from the vault.
It did NOT answer the beat question from general knowledge or invent reported figures for the quarter.
It DID report the model's expectations from the vault (Base Year-1 revenue growth 10% and NOPAT margin 17%, and that Widgets growth is the key watchlist metric) as the 'what we expected' half.
It offered the concrete next step: run bav-update on ACME to pull the quarter and do the forecast-vs-actual diff (or an explicit live check if the user wants one now).
No file under coverage/ was created, modified, or deleted.
